summary: Descriptive overview of righttoprivacy in substantive law: contraception, abortion (pre/post-Dobbs), marriage.
key_points: contraception is a fundamental concept in constrights law.
Legal standards for righttoprivacy are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine righttoprivacy in litigation.
The doctrine of righttoprivacy has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding righttoprivacy requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
